name: Banská Bystrica Castle
description: An urban fortified complex in the heart of Banská Bystrica comprising the Church of the Virgin Mary, Matthias House, a Slovak church, the town hall and stone fortifications with bastions. Unlike royal castles it served purely as a fortification protecting the mining town's patrician class and royal treasury.

name: Betliar Mansion
description: A country manor house originally built on a 15th-century Bebek castle site, rebuilt by the Andrássy family in 1880 into a luxurious residence. Now an exposition of the Slovak National Museum, it contains art, historical furniture, an extensive library, weapons, ceramics and the mummified remains of an Egyptian man from the Eighteenth Dynasty.

name: Biely Kameň
description: Gothic castle ruins of large spatial extent on a forested rocky hill of the Little Carpathians above Svätý Jur. The site is actively maintained by the local heritage association Castrum Sancti Georgii.

name: Blatnica
description: Castle ruin at 658 m above sea level on a limestone ridge between Gaderská Valley and Turčianska Basin, about 2 km from Blatnica village; under active reconstruction.

name: Bojnice Castle
description: First documented in 1113, Bojnice Castle was comprehensively reconstructed in a Romantic style between 1889 and 1910 under Count János Ferenc Pálfi. Slovakia's most visited castle and museum, it features a natural travertine cave, a formal park, and the approximately 700-year-old Linden Tree of King Matthias.

name: Branč
description: One of the oldest castles in the Záhorie region, situated on a slope of the Myjavská Pahorkatina plateau above the village of Podbranč. Partially, not very sensitively restored ruins.

name: Bratislava Castle
description: Bratislava Castle is the dominant landmark of the Slovak capital, perched on a rock above the left bank of the Danube. Repeatedly rebuilt over the centuries, its current four-towered form houses a branch of the Slovak National Museum.

name: Brezovica
description: A defunct small castle (hrádok) in Brezovica village. Only the hilltop site remains; a chapel now stands where the fortification once was.

name: Budatín Castle
description: Originally a lowland castle at the confluence of the Váh and Kysuce rivers in Žilina; now houses the Považské Museum.

name: Budmerice Mansion
description: An eclectic manor house built in 1889 for the Pálffy family, designed by Viennese architect Franz von Neumann with inspiration from Loire Valley châteaux. Set in an English landscape park, it was used by the Association of Slovak Writers from the 1950s until 2011 and is now administered by the Slovak Ministry of Culture.

name: Bytča Castle
description: Originally a medieval fortress transformed into a Renaissance residence; contains Slovakia's only wedding palace (sobášny palác) and was historically linked to the Elizabeth Báthory trials (1611); under reconstruction as of 2021.

name: Bzovík
description: A former Premonstratensian monastery converted into a gothic-renaissance anti-Turkish fortress. The site preserves the final remnants of the former monastery surrounded by strong walls and four bastions. Freely accessible and family-friendly.

name: Čabraď
description: Ruins of a gothic castle at the end of a rock promontory encircled on three sides by the Litava River. The site is maintained by the local association Rondel. It offers surprising size and an interesting location with scenic vistas.

name: Castrum Salis
description: An Árpádian-era frontier stronghold whose name derives from its role in the salt trade. A Baroque Church of St. Stephen of Hungary now stands on the site.

name: Čeklís
description: One of the least-known castles in Slovakia, located on the edge of Bernolákovo village. Only minimal fragmentary remains have survived above ground.

name: Červený Kameň Castle
description: A well-preserved Renaissance castle near the village of Častá in the Little Carpathians, operated as a museum by the Slovak National Museum. The castle offers multiple tour routes and is known as one of Slovakia's finest castle attractions.

name: Červený Kláštor
description: A former Carthusian monastery founded in the 14th century on the Dunajec River in the Pieniny region. Secularised in 1782 under Emperor Joseph II and no longer an active religious institution. The complex is a national cultural monument administered in connection with Pieniny National Park and now operates as a museum open to visitors.

name: Čierny hrad
description: Čierny hrad (Black Castle) sits on a wooded ridge of the Tribeč Mountains near the village of Zlatno, historically associated with gold mining in the region. Very little of the original structure survives beyond a prominent earth rampart. The site is freely accessible via a yellow-marked trail from Zlatno.

name: Devín Castle
description: Devín Castle sits on a limestone cliff above the confluence of the Danube and Morava rivers, featuring preserved ruins of Renaissance palaces, a 60-meter-deep well, and a tower called Mníška (Virgin's Tower). Originally fortified with Roman and Slavic origins, the castle was built as a stone fortress in the 13th century and expanded over subsequent centuries before being damaged by Napoleon's troops in 1809.

name: Divín
description: A partially preserved castle ruin on a wooded hill directly above the village of Divín. The site includes a manor house and fortified church, and features a newly discovered cave beneath the castle. Paid admission, open daily 9:00–17:00.

name: Dobrá Niva
description: The site preserves visible remnants of ramparts and one consolidated castle wall above the village of Podzámčok. The location offers beautiful views and is visible from the Zvolen–Krupina road. Freely accessible.

name: Dobrá Voda
description: Quite extensive castle structure well hidden in the forest near the village of the same name, surrounded by woodland in the Little Carpathians.

name: Dolná Krupá Manor
description: A classicist manor house, one of the residences of the noble House of Chotek, associated with Beethoven who reportedly composed here. Now a branch of the Slovak National Museum – Music Museum. The estate features a notable rosarium created by Countess Marie Henrietta Chotek von Wognin.

name: Dolná Mičiná
description: A Renaissance mansion built on a gothic foundation in Dolná Mičiná village, in the Zvolen hills near the river Lukavica. The structure is privately owned and poorly maintained, with hazardous conditions discouraging entry.

name: Dračí hrádok
description: A small medieval fortification whose remnants are spread along a forested ridge at the end of a tongue of land extending into the Červený potok valley, about 2 km east of Borinka village.

name: Dúbravica
description: A heavily ruined and overgrown fortified residence or manor in the centre of Dúbravica village. The structure historically featured a guard tower. No archaeological research has yet been conducted on the site.

name: Dvorníky – Posádka hrádok I.
description: Defunct fortification occupying the northern section of Posádka municipality; much of the former castle site is now used as a cemetery.

name: Fiľakovský hrad
description: One of Slovakia's oldest masonry castles built on a volcanic hill above Fiľakovo. The castle is largely in ruins but the Bebekov Tower has been reconstructed and houses a historical exhibition. It was destroyed by Turkish forces in 1680 after a month-long siege.

name: Gelnica
description: Gelnica Castle stood on a strategic hill above the mining town of Gelnica, guarding the surrounding ore-rich region. It burned down at the end of the 17th century and its masonry was later reused to build the town hall. Today only the last remnants of walls and a tower section survive, accessible via Zámková Street.

name: Gerulata
description: Gerulata was an important Roman auxiliary fort forming part of the Danubian limes defensive system, located in the Rusovce district of Bratislava. The site is administered by the Museum of the City of Bratislava and features an on-site museum.

name: Glanzeberg
description: A lesser-known castle ruin overlooking the town of Banská Štiavnica from above. The site offers scenic views of the local calvary and is reached via a short hike from the Hodruša Hámre direction. Freely accessible.

name: Gýmeš
description: Gýmeš is the ruin of an originally Gothic castle perched on a quartzite hill of the Tribeč Mountains above the village of Jelenec near Nitra. It remained in use until the mid-19th century and is considered one of the most scenic castle ruins in Slovakia. The site is freely accessible via marked hiking trails from Kostolany pod Tribečom.

name: Halič Castle
description: A Renaissance castle first mentioned in literature in 1450. Rebuilt in 1762 by the Forgáč family under architect Andre Meyerhoffer. Sold in 2006 to a development company from Košice who restored it as a 4-star hotel with a spa.

name: Hlohovec Castle
description: A Renaissance-Baroque château built in 1720 by the Erdődy family on the site of an earlier medieval castle, forming the dominant landmark of Hlohovec. The complex includes an Empire-style theatre from 1802, an 18th-century riding school, a baroque garden pavilion, and a castle park with French terraces.

name: Holumnica
description: Modest wall remnants of a possible fortified aristocratic residence located directly within the village of Holumnica. Historical records question its classification as a proper castle.

name: Hrad Blh
description: A castle ruin located within a game preserve near Veľký Blh in the Rimavská Sobota district. The site maintains impressive dimensions and receives minimal visitor traffic. Vehicle access requires authorisation; on foot approximately 50–60 minutes from the gate.

name: Hrad Breznica
description: The last remains of a medieval castle near Tekovská Breznica, part of the Pohronská hradná cesta castle route. A marked green trail leads from the cemetery parking area through zigzag paths to the ruins. The route continues to the nearby Sokolia Skala fortlet.

name: Hrad Hajnáčka
description: A hilltop ruin built on a volcanic outcrop near Hajnáčka village. Little has been preserved beyond the last remnants of walls and one surviving chamber with a carved staircase. Freely accessible with an iconic gate entrance and panoramic views.

name: Hrad Hodejov
description: A heavily overgrown medieval fortification above Hodejov village, between Rimavská Sobota and Fiľakovo. The castle was damaged by historical quarrying and dismantling by local inhabitants. Freely accessible via a short 10-minute hike from the cemetery.

name: Hrad Lietava
description: One of Slovakia's most impressive castle ruins in the Súľov Mountains, approximately 10 km from Žilina; maintained by a local association with a small exhibition and refreshments.

name: Hrad Lipovce
description: A former royal castle on Jazvečí Hill above Lačnovská gorge, built to guard the trade route from Prešov to Levoča. A large stone tower and final wall fragments remain.

name: Hrad Liptovský Hrádok
description: Originally a guard castle at the intersection of two major trade routes; the older nucleus became a ruin after an 1803 fire while the younger manor house survives; now operates as Hotel Chateau GrandCastle.

name: Hrad Peťuša
description: A defunct medieval castle presumed to date from the late 13th century, likely built as a toll station guarding important trade routes in the Zvolen district. Recent archaeological work uncovered some wall remnants, though these have since been reburied. No historical documentation of its original name survives.

name: Hrad Slovenská Ľupča
description: A castle undergoing ongoing restoration above the village of Slovenská Ľupča, forming a dominant landmark visible from the Banská Bystrica–Brezno road. Open to the public with guided tours of approximately 30–40 minutes.

name: Hrad Teplica
description: Also known as Pustý hrad or Červený hrad, this castle above Sklené Teplice guarded access routes to Banská Štiavnica. It was dismantled around the turn of the 16th–17th centuries and its stones used to construct a local church. Freely accessible via marked trails from Sklené Teplice spa town.

name: Hrad Zniev (horný, starší)
description: The upper and older part of Zniev castle at the hilltop; practically nothing remains, with building material likely reused in the lower castle.

name: Hradisko Bojná Valy
description: Hradisko Bojná Valy is an early medieval fortified settlement near Bojná village in the Topoľčany district, featuring massive earth ramparts several meters high and a reconstructed entrance gate. The site is freely accessible on foot and is valued for its well-preserved defensive earthworks. Vehicle access is restricted after 17:00.

name: Hradisko Dražovce
description: Hradisko Dražovce is a prehistoric fortified settlement above the village of Dražovce in the Nitra district. An iconic Romanesque chapel now stands on the site of the former hillfort, and visible earthwork remnants can still be observed. The site is freely accessible and suitable for family visits.

name: Hradisko Ducové
description: Great Moravian-era fortified settlement between Ducové and Hubina villages near Piešťany. Features one of the best-preserved Great Moravian church/rotunda foundations and reconstructed palisades on earthen ramparts.

name: Hradisko Stráňa
description: Archaeological hillfort site in Ludrová (Ružomberok district); offers views across to the opposite hill where Hrádok Páncová once stood.

name: Hradisko Žiar
description: Prehistoric hillfort above Sedliacka Dubová village (Dolný Kubín district) near a ruined church; history reaches back to prehistory.

name: Hrádok Dolné Janíky
description: Star-shaped fortress near Dolné Janíky village with clearly visible fortification ramparts, situated metres from the Malý Dunaj river.

name: Hrádok Jalšové
description: Ruined small castle in Jalšové village where St. Martin's Church now stands on the former castle location; a preserved moat remains visible around the church grounds.

name: Hrádok Malé Ostré
description: Extinct fortification in Kysucké Nové Mesto district; largely destroyed by quarrying after 1985 archaeological finds of pottery, an arrowhead, and a spear point.

name: Hrádok Páncová
description: Ruined small castle on a steep bare hilltop above Ludrová near Ružomberok; only faint terrain traces remain of the original structure.

name: Hrádok Podhorany
description: Hrádok Podhorany is a disappeared medieval small castle (hrádok) in the village of Podhorany, Nitra district. The site is on fenced private land and is not accessible to the public. Adjacent ruins of a fortified church are also present in the vicinity.

name: Hričovský hrad
description: Ruins on a distinctive cliff of the northernmost outcrop of the Súľov Hills at 564 m above sea level above Hričovské Podhradie; access via red-marked trail.

name: Hronský Beňadik Abbey
description: A fortified Benedictine monastery founded in 1075 by King Géza I of Hungary as Monasterium Ecclesia Sancti Benedicti. The Nitra Gospels, the oldest Latin book from the territory of Slovakia, were written here around 1100. A national cultural monument since 1963, housing 13th-century sculptures and 14th-century Madonna figures.

name: Hrušov
description: Hrušov castle stands on the top of a quartzite ridge of the Tribeč Mountains south of Skýcov village in the Zlaté Moravce district. The ruins are maintained by the Leustach association, which keeps the grounds in good condition. The site is freely accessible with parking available near the main road.

name: Humenné Mansion
description: A former residence of the Druget dynasty built in Renaissance style around 1610 and later rebuilt in Neo-Baroque style. One of the largest manor-type buildings in Slovakia, now housing the Vihorlat Museum with over a hundred thousand exhibits including Hungarian Royal Gallery pieces and Celtic-Dacian coin treasures.

name: Jedľové Kostoľany (Živanská veža)
description: Živanská veža (Živany Tower) is a ruined medieval watchtower near Jedľové Kostoľany in the Zlaté Moravce district, built to guard an important trade route. Only the northern part of the original tower has survived. The structure likely fell into disuse after 1708, around the same time as nearby Hrušov castle.

name: Kamenica
description: Medieval castle ruins on a limestone ridge in the Čergov Mountains above Kamenica village. The fortress resisted the Hussites; Imperial forces later destroyed it and locals quarried its stones for a distillery.

name: Kapušiansky hrad
description: Originally called Tobul, this royal guard castle above Kapušany village served as a watchtower. Burned by order of the Hungarian Diet in 1715, it is now undergoing restoration with a tower open to visitors.

name: Kaštieľ Borša
description: Built in 1570 on the site of an earlier medieval water castle, the manor at Borša is historically significant as the birthplace of Ferenc II Rákóczi in 1676. The building has been fully restored and now operates as a museum with interactive exhibits.

name: Kaštieľ Kotešová
description: Originally a water fortification converted into a manor house in Kotešová (Bytča district); privately owned and available for accommodation rental.

name: Kaštieľ Lukáčovce
description: Kaštieľ Lukáčovce is a Renaissance-era fortified manor house situated along the main road through Lukáčovce village in the Nitra district. First mentioned in the 15th century, the structure is currently closed and in a deteriorating state. It lies close to the Hrádok Lukáčovce motte site.

name: Kaštieľ Radoľa
description: Renaissance square residential tower in Radoľa (Kysucké Nové Mesto district) with cross vaults and comb ornamentation; houses a Kysucké Museum exhibition.

name: Kaštieľ Vécseyovcov
description: This Renaissance manor with an L-shaped floor plan stands on the site of a medieval fortress in Streda nad Bodrogom. Some medieval architectural elements remain visible within the later Renaissance construction. The building currently houses the local municipal office.

name: Kazimír
description: The castle at Kazimír left no surviving written records of its origins, and only a single wall fragment — likely from a palace building — survives beside the local church above the Izra stream. The remnants can be viewed via a small pavilion and stairway near the site.

name: Kežmarok Castle
description: The only completely preserved castle in the Spiš region, first documented in 1463. Houses multiple exhibitions on the development of Kežmarok city.

name: Komárno Fortress
description: One of the largest 19th-century fortress complexes in Central Europe. The Old Fortress and New Fortress on the Slovak side of the Danube form a bastion-type defensive system stretching from the 16th through late 19th centuries. The Old Fortress is considered the first fortress of its type in Central Europe.

name: Korlátka
description: Gothic castle ruins on a rocky outcrop in the Little Carpathians near Cerová (Rozbehy), notable for being one of the few castles approached by going downhill. Relatively large ruins with scenic vistas.

name: Kotrčiná Lúčka I.
description: Ruined small castle above Kotrčiná Lúčka village (Žilina district) with well-preserved earthen ramparts and scenic views of the village.

name: Kotrčiná Lúčka II.
description: Secondary ruined fortification above Kotrčiná Lúčka village (Žilina district); best visited together with Kotrčiná Lúčka I in a single excursion.

name: Krásna Hôrka Castle
description: A Gothic castle first recorded in 1333, held by the Andrássy family from 1578 to 1918. One of Slovakia's best-preserved medieval castles until severely damaged by fire on 10 March 2012, which destroyed the roof, the Gothic palace exhibition area, and the bell tower. Managed by the Slovak National Museum; restoration is ongoing.

name: Kremnica Town Castle
description: A medieval fortified castle complex built from the 13th through 15th centuries, described as a unique example of a very well-preserved fortification system in Central Europe. The multifunctional complex includes St. Catherine's Church and is associated with the National Bank of Slovakia's Museum of Coins and Medals.
ownedBy: NBS – Museum of Coins and Medals

name: Leopoldov Fortress
description: A star-shaped artillery fortress founded in 1664–1669 on the initiative of Emperor Leopold I as a defence against the Ottoman Turks. Since 1855 it has functioned as a high-security state prison and is not open to the public.

name: Levice
description: Levice castle is situated in the center of the town of Levice at the intersection of the Hronská floodplain and Ipeľ hilly country. The castle complex includes an adjacent manor house (kaštieľ) that houses the Tekovské Museum. The grounds are open to the public daily.

name: Likava
description: The largest castle in the Liptov region above Likava village; a royal guard castle with two accessible courtyards and Hunyad Tower; managed by Liptov Museum.

name: Ľubovňa Castle
description: A border guard fortress overlooking Stará Ľubovňa. Notable for housing Polish coronation jewels; replicas are on display. Open year-round with attractive exhibitions.

name: Malé Šance hviezdicová reduta
description: Star-shaped redoubt forming part of the approximately 1,800 m long Malé Šance fortification system in Čadca district; enlarged in 1664; historically garrisoned by 40 men.

name: Malé Šance malá reduta
description: Small redoubt forming part of the 17th-century Malé Šance fortification complex in Čadca district; heavily overgrown but the earthworks remain identifiable.

name: Malinovo Castle
description: A classicist four-wing manor house evolved from a medieval moated castle first mentioned in 1209. Reconstructed after 1677 from a water castle into a Renaissance manor, then modernised in the early 19th century by the Apponyi family. Portions of the original moat survive. Currently houses the Combined Secondary School of Horticulture named after Gustáv Čejka and is in need of renovation.

name: Markušovce Manor House
description: A manor house built in 1643 for the Máriássy family, now operating as a museum and branch of the Spiš Museum. The grounds include the rococo 'Dardanely' belvedere from 1778 housing a collection of historical musical instruments regularly used for concerts. The earlier Máriássy castle on the property dates to 1284 and was abandoned after a fire in 1773.

name: Medzianky
description: A small fortified castle (hrádok) without a forecastle, initially commissioned by the Johannite Order and used by the Hussite Brethren in the mid-15th century. Situated on a rocky promontory with panoramic views.

name: Modrý Kameň
description: A gothic castle ruin with a preserved Baroque manor house directly above the town of Modrý Kameň in the Krupina Plateau. The Baroque manor now houses a toy museum. The site offers scenic views of the surrounding landscape.

name: Nezbudská Lúčka
description: Ruined small castle on a hill beside a railway underpass in Nezbudská Lúčka (Žilina district); partially destroyed by railway construction; a chapel now occupies the site.

name: Nitra Castle
description: Nitra Castle is the dominant landmark of the city of Nitra, situated on a limestone hill at 220 meters above sea level. It is one of Slovakia's oldest and best-preserved castle complexes, incorporating a cathedral that has been the seat of the Diocese of Nitra for over a millennium. Entry to the castle grounds is free, while cathedral tours require a ticket.

name: Nový Zámok
description: A Renaissance watchtower fortress built in the 16th century as part of Banská Štiavnica's fortifications against Ottoman raids. Known as the 'New Castle' in contrast to the older town castle, it has been transformed into a museum and is administered by the Slovak Mining Museum.

name: Obliehacie opevnenie hradu Teplica
description: A small siege fortification located approximately 350 metres south of Hrad Teplica, with recognisable earthwork ramparts. It functioned as an auxiliary military outpost during the castle's active period. Freely accessible via the green-marked trail heading south from Teplica Castle.

name: Opevnenie mesta Sabinov
description: City fortifications of the royal free town of Sabinov, construction ordered in 1474. Originally over 1,300 m of walls with 16 towers; only fragments on Ružová street survive today.

name: Opevnený kostol – Šenkvice
description: A Gothic church of St. Anna in Šenkvice enclosed by a Renaissance rectangular defensive wall with corner bastions, built during the period of Ottoman incursions. The exterior and well-maintained grounds are freely accessible to visitors.

name: Oponický hrad
description: Oponický hrad is a ruined castle perched on a wooded quartzite rock southeast of the village of Oponice in the Tribeč Mountains, Topoľčany district. The ruins are considered among the most scenic in the Tribeč range and are freely accessible via a green-marked hiking trail. A picnic area is available near the castle.

name: Orava Castle
description: Dominant landmark of the Orava region above the Orava River in Oravský Podzámok; one of Slovakia's most beautiful castles with multiple museum tour routes operated by Orava Museum.

name: Ostrý Kameň
description: Castle ruins located on limestone cliffs of the Záruby ridge above Buková village near Smolenice, at 528 metres elevation. Features extensive remains with fireplaces and informational plaques.

name: Pajštún
description: A large and scenic medieval castle ruin perched on a limestone rock in the Little Carpathians above the village of Borinka. The castle was destroyed by French forces in 1809 and is today one of the most visited castle ruins near Bratislava.

name: Parič
description: Parič was a lowland water castle, formerly surrounded by moats, located in what is now a Trebišov city park. Only the western portion of a brick wall survives, the rest having been destroyed during feudal uprisings with the stone repurposed for a nearby manor house.

name: Pezinok – Starý hrad II
description: Fragmentary medieval castle remains north of Pezinok, of which a significant portion was lost to quarrying operations. The site offers pleasant views and is best visited as a brief stop when touring the nearby Červený Kameň Castle.

name: Pezinok – Starý zámok I
description: A defunct medieval fortification above Pezinok town, surviving only as earthen ramparts and terraces on a forested hillside above a psychiatric hospital. Wild boar are present in the surrounding forest.

name: Pezinok Castle
description: A castle originating as a moated fortress between 1231 and 1271, later transformed into a Renaissance chateau. Long associated with the Pálffy family. Its cellars house the National Wine Salon, and the property now operates as a hotel, making it a centre of Pezinok's wine culture heritage.

name: Plaveč
description: A royal stone castle above the village of Plaveč, first recorded in the late 13th century. Lost military significance in the 18th century and suffered fire damage in the 19th. Reconstruction work ongoing since 2014.

name: Plavecký hrad
description: A large castle ruin on a marginal limestone hill of the Little Carpathians directly above the village of Plavecké Podhradie, commanding expansive views over the Záhorie lowland. The site is freely accessible and popular with families.

name: Plešivec
description: Only a fragmentary section of the castle wall of Plešivec survives, located within a private residential garden on the edge of the Slovenský Kras karst region. The site is on private property and is viewable only from outside the fence.

name: Podolínec – mestský hrad
description: Urban castle of the historic royal city of Podolínec. The original palace was replaced by the municipal office building; medieval fortifications remain preserved on Baštová Street.

name: Prietrž hradby
description: Fortifications of uncertain origin with a trapezoid ground plan and 61 shooting ports, locally called Hussite fortifications. May have served as anti-Turkish defences; exact historical origins unknown.

name: Pustý hrad
description: One of the largest castle complexes in Slovakia, consisting of upper and lower castles above Zvolen. Among the oldest medieval castles in Slovakia, with the lower castle discovered relatively recently. A buffet operates between the two levels during summer.

name: Radoľa – arch. lokalita
description: Archaeological site on a hill above Radoľa village (Kysucké Nové Mesto district) containing remains of an early Gothic chapel and a Hussite fortified structure.

name: Rajecký hrad
description: Only the last stone wall remnants confirmed by archaeological research survive near Dubová hill above Jasenové (Žilina district); educational trail and a lookout tower on site.

name: Revište
description: A medieval fortress on a steep andesite hill above the Hron River, managed by the Association for the Rescue of Revište Castle since 2011. Active restoration is ongoing and the site hosts regular cultural events. An educational trail with eight information panels leads from the free parking area.

name: Richnava
description: The last remnants of Richnava castle are found along an inconspicuous alley in the village, with wall sections straddling the boundary between a private residence and the public street. Most of the original stonework was removed and repurposed for the construction of the nearby Kluknava manor. An information plaque marks the site.

name: Sandberg
description: A well-known archaeological site with educational trails near Bratislava, featuring the still-discernible earthwork rampart of a medieval fortification situated above a former quarry. The site pairs naturally with a visit to nearby Devín Castle.

name: Šášov
description: A partially preserved medieval fortress above the Hron River, maintained by the Association for the Rescue of Šášov Castle since 2003. A significant portion of the structure survives with ongoing volunteer restoration. The castle hosts periodic guided night tours.

name: Šebeš
description: A small medieval castle above Podhradík village near Prešov on an andesite hill, rebuilt from wood to stone. Only final wall remnants survive; deliberately burned by the residents and magistrate of Prešov.

name: Sitno
description: Castle ruins occupying the site of a former fortified settlement on Sitno hill at 860 metres in the Štiavnice Mountains, offering some of the most scenic castle views in Slovakia. Two hiking routes exist: an easier asphalt road from Svätý Anton or a more challenging marked trail from Lake Počúvadlo. Freely accessible.

name: Sklabiňa
description: Largest fortress in the Turiec region above Sklabinský Podzámok (Martin district); cared for by the DONJON association with a small museum, local cheese and sausage sales.

name: Smolenice Castle
description: Originally built in the 15th century and destroyed in regional conflicts, the present Romantic castle was reconstructed in the early 20th century under Count József Pálffy, drawing inspiration from Kreuzenstein castle near Vienna. Acquired by the state in 1945 and managed by the Slovak Academy of Sciences as a conference centre since 1953. Open to the public in July and August.

name: Šomoška
description: A castle ruin on a conical basalt hill in the Cerová vrchovina range on the Slovak–Hungarian border. The Slovak approach features an interesting stone waterfall and stone sea geological formations. The site is very photogenic with views of the nearby Hungarian castle Salgó vára.

name: Soví hrad
description: Practically nothing has been preserved of this castle above Šurice village; only a cistern and stone-carved steps remain on a rare rock formation. The site features a pavilion and informational signs with a short 10–15 minute woodland trail to the summit. Freely accessible.

name: Spiš Castle
description: Spiš Castle is one of the largest castle complexes in Central Europe, perched on a dramatic travertine hill in eastern Slovakia and inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List. The oldest surviving element is a circular residential tower, with major expansions occurring in the 13th–15th centuries. After a fire in 1780 the garrison withdrew and the castle fell into ruin; it is now managed by the Slovak National Museum as an open-air heritage site.

name: Spišský Štvrtok
description: A fortified church of St. Ladislaus with surrounding defensive walls and an earthen rampart visible on the northern side, located at the centre of Spišský Štvrtok village.

name: Staré Šance
description: Square-plan fortress in Svrčinovec (Kysúc region) at 630 m elevation with surviving ditch; archaeological finds include pottery fragments and pipes; accessible by car.

name: Starhrad
description: One of Slovakia's most beautiful castle ruins forming a distinctive landmark above the Váh River near Varín; maintained by the Castrum Warna association.

name: Starý Zámok
description: A municipal castle in the centre of Banská Štiavnica created by the reconstruction of a fortified church. The castle houses multiple exhibitions including mining displays, municipal history, and weapons collections. Managed by the regional museum.

name: Strážky Manor
description: A Renaissance manor in Spišská Belá with origins in a 12th-13th century defensive structure, expanded by the Horváth-Stansith family in the 16th-17th centuries. Designated a national cultural monument in 1963 and restored in the 1960s–1990s. Now a branch of the Slovak National Gallery housing four permanent exhibitions including works of László Mednyánszky.

name: Strečno Castle
description: Reconstructed ruin on a rocky cliff of Malá Fatra above the Váh River; operated by Považské Museum; exceptional panoramic views of the Váh valley.

name: Studený hrad
description: Studený hrad was an 11th-century wooden fortification in the Nitra district that had already disappeared by the 13th century, leaving no significant visible remains. The hilltop site offers panoramic views of the surrounding countryside and the nearby Gýmeš castle. It is freely accessible via hiking trails from Jelenec.

name: Stupava Mansion
description: A Renaissance château originally built as a water castle and rebuilt in the 17th century. Associated with the Pálffy noble family. Currently functions as a social services facility.

name: Sučiansky hrad
description: Castle ruin in the hills above Podhradie (Martin district) accessed via a green-marked trail along Hradský stream; practically nothing of the structure survives.

name: Súľovský hrad
description: One of Slovakia's least accessible castles on a conglomerate hill at 660 m in the Súľov Mountains (Bytča district); originally consisting of lower and upper sections; trail includes ladders.

name: Svätý Anton Mansion
description: A Baroque manor house significantly expanded in the mid-18th century under Earl Andrej Kohary into a four-wing noble residence. The last private owner was Ferdinand I of Bulgaria. Since 1962 it has operated as a museum of art, history, and hunting, housing collections from the 18th to 20th centuries from around the world, set within a baroque park.

name: Topoľčiansky hrad
description: Topoľčiansky hrad is a ruined castle rising above the village of Podhradie on a partially wooded dolomite hill in the Považský Inovec range, Topoľčany district. It serves as the dominant landmark of the surrounding area and is visible from a considerable distance. Seasonal free access is available via a short trail from the village church.

name: Tornaľský hrad
description: The original small fortified tower at this site no longer exists; an artificially created folly ruin was constructed in the 19th century in its place. Located above an amphitheatre in the municipal park of Tornaľa. Freely accessible via a short 10-minute walk from parking near the local château.

name: Veľký Kamenec
description: Veľký Kamenec castle was likely built after the 13th-century Mongol invasion on a rhyolite hill rising from the East Slovakian plain. The castle fell into ruin at the end of the 17th century when imperial forces destroyed it. The freely accessible ruins carry warning signs about falling stone hazards.

name: Vígľaš Castle
description: A robust reconstructed castle set on a low hill spur of the Javorie range, surrounded by the Slatina River in Vígľaš village near Zvolen. Comprehensively restored and now operated as a luxury hotel.

name: Vodný hrad Hronsek
description: A water castle in Hronsek village undergoing reconstruction, accompanied by a wooden church and manor house within the same settlement. Located directly in the village and easy to find.

name: Zbojnícky hrad
description: A stone fortress originally built to protect nearby salt mines, rebuilt from an earlier wooden structure. Demolished by regional assembly in 1715. Despite the popular name meaning 'Bandit Castle', no confirmed outlaw activity is documented. Restoration ongoing.

name: Zvolen Castle
description: A preserved originally gothic castle with renaissance superstructure in the centre of Zvolen city, operated by the Slovak National Gallery. The castle is a national cultural monument hosting gallery exhibitions and available for event rental.
